sections.markdown:
# One-armed Conditionals: WHEN and UNLESS

`if` wants exactly one form per branch. When you want to do several things **only if** a test is true, reach for `when`:

```lisp
(when test
  body1
  body2
  ...)
```

It's equivalent to `(if test (progn body...))` — an implicit `progn`, and no else branch. If the test is false, `when` returns `NIL`. If true, it returns the last body form's value.

`unless` is the mirror image — it runs the body **only if** the test is `NIL`:

```lisp
(unless test
  body...)
```

Style tip: if you're tempted to write `(if test nil something)`, use `(unless test something)` instead.

sections.explain: Test is false; WHEN returns NIL.
ZEROP is NIL for 5, so UNLESS runs its body.
All three body forms are evaluated; the last one's value is returned.

sections.prompt: Define AT-LEAST-TEN that returns N if N is 10 or greater, otherwise NIL.
Define NONZERO-DOUBLE that returns 2*N if N is not zero, otherwise NIL. Use UNLESS.
